items.mistake: Fitting StandardScaler or SimpleImputer on the full dataset before train_test_split
items.fix: Split first, then pipeline.fit(X_train, y_train); use pipeline.predict(X_test) so all statistics come from training rows only.
items.why: Holdout rows influence medians and variances, leaking future information and inflating offline metrics.
items.example: Calling `scaler.fit_transform(pd.concat([X_train, X_test]))` before split lets test incomes change the scaler mean seen during training.

items.mistake: Scaling numeric columns before imputing NaN values
items.fix: Order numeric branch as imputer → scaler inside the Pipeline or ColumnTransformer.
items.why: StandardScaler cannot compute finite mean and variance on NaN; wrong order causes errors or hidden fill steps.
items.example: Pipeline([('scaler', StandardScaler()), ('imputer', SimpleImputer())]) fails or forces inconsistent handling of gaps.

items.mistake: Running pandas dedupe and string normalization only in the training notebook, not in the serving path
items.fix: Wrap hygiene in FunctionTransformer or a custom transformer inside the same Pipeline saved to joblib.
items.why: Train/serve skew: the model trains on stripped uppercase cities but production sends raw `" usa"` strings to the encoder.
items.example: Training notebook runs `df['city'].str.strip()` manually; the API passes unstripped values and OneHotEncoder treats them as unseen categories.

items.mistake: Refitting the imputer on each production batch to "adapt" to new data
items.fix: Load the fitted pipeline artifact and call predict only; schedule explicit retraining jobs that refit on curated training snapshots.
items.why: Batch refit changes fill values and encoder vocabularies without version control, breaking reproducibility and leaking live traffic into parameters.
items.example: A nightly job calls `pipeline.fit(latest_batch)` before predict, so Monday's medians differ from Sunday's for the same customer row.
